content.text: Prometheus config only scrapes itself. Let me verify the metrics endpoint works and check the Prometheus deployment.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c
echo "===metrics from rabbitmq-0==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s http://rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692/metrics | head -5
echo "===rabbitmq_up sample==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s http://rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692/metrics | grep -i rabbitmq_up
echo "===prom deploy==="
kubectl -n $NS get deploy prometheus -o yaml | grep -A40 "containers:"

content.content:
===metrics from rabbitmq-0===
# TYPE erlang_mnesia_held_locks gauge
# HELP erlang_mnesia_held_locks Number of held locks.
erlang_mnesia_held_locks 0
# TYPE erlang_mnesia_lock_queue gauge
# HELP erlang_mnesia_lock_queue Number of transactions waiting for a lock.
===rabbitmq_up sample===
===prom deploy===
      containers:
      - args:
        - --config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
        - --storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus
        - --web.enable-lifecycle
        image: prom/prometheus:v2.51.2
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: prometheus
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9090
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /etc/prometheus/
          name: config
        - mountPath: /prometheus
          name: data
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          name: prometheus-config
        name: config
      - emptyDir: {}
        name: data
status:
  availableReplicas: 1
  conditions:
  - lastTransitionTime: "2026-06-18T10:04:39Z"
    lastUpdateTime: "2026-06-18T10:04:39Z"

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s http://rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692/metrics | grep -iE "^[a-z_]*up[ {]" | head

content.text: Confirmed: the native plugin exposes no `rabbitmq_up` metric. I'll add a scrape job for the 3 pods plus a recording rule defining `rabbitmq_up` from `up{job="rabbitmq"}`. Let me write the new config.

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-config
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 15s
    rule_files:
      - /etc/prometheus/rules.yml
    scrape_configs:
      - job_name: "prometheus"
        static_configs:
          - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
      - job_name: "rabbitmq"
        static_configs:
          - targets:
              - "rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
              - "rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
              - "rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
  rules.yml: |
    groups:
      - name: rabbitmq
        rules:
          - record: rabbitmq_up
            expr: up{job="rabbitmq"}

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-config
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 15s
    rule_files:
      - /etc/prometheus/rules.yml
    scrape_configs:
      - job_name: "prometheus"
        static_configs:
          - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
      - job_name: "rabbitmq"
        static_configs:
          - targets:
              - "rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
              - "rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
              - "rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
  rules.yml: |
    groups:
      - name: rabbitmq
        rules:
          - record: rabbitmq_up
            expr: up{job="rabbitmq"}

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-config
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 15s
    rule_files:
      - /etc/prometheus/rules.yml
    scrape_configs:
      - job_name: "prometheus"
        static_configs:
          - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
      - job_name: "rabbitmq"
        static_configs:
          - targets:
              - "rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
              - "rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
              - "rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c.svc.cluster.local:15692"
  rules.yml: |
    groups:
      - name: rabbitmq
        rules:
          - record: rabbitmq_up
            expr: up{job="rabbitmq"}

kubectl apply -f /tmp/prom-config.yaml
kubectl -n $NS rollout restart deploy/prometheus
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deploy/prometheus --timeout=120s

content.text: I restarted Prometheus to pick up the new config (a hot reload also works, but restart is clean). Now let me wait for scrapes and verify.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-manual-monitoring-2026-a336b76c
PROM=http://prometheus.$NS.svc.cluster.local:8000
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  n=$(kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s "$PROM/api/v1/query?query=rabbitmq_up" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '"value"' | wc -l)
  echo "attempt $i: rabbitmq_up samples=$n"
  [ "$n" -ge 3 ] && break
  sleep 6
done
echo "===TARGETS==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s "$PROM/api/v1/targets" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(t['labels'].get('job'), t['scrapeUrl'], t['health']) for t in d['data']['activeTargets']]"
echo "===rabbitmq_up query==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s "$PROM/api/v1/query?query=rabbitmq_up" | python3 -m json.tool
